description: Modernize homepage: add SVG KREEP logo with glitch animation in navbar and hero, implement cart dropdown overlay, make New Arrivals section functional with size/inventory display, and wire up 'Submit yours' to studio.html
steps: Create an inline SVG logo for KREEP in the navbar (.nav-logo in all 3 HTML files) and in the hero section of index.html. The SVG should depict: a gray 'K' shape with dusty-pink (#D6A5A5) swirl accent, and 'KREEP' text with the 'E' in dusty pink (#D6A5A5). Apply the existing .glitch class and data-text attribute to the SVG wrapper so the glitch animation fires on it. The SVG should be compact (around 40px height in navbar, larger in hero).
Replace the text-only nav-logo ('KREEP') in index.html, studio.html, and product.html with the new SVG logo wrapped in the glitch animation container. Keep the nav-logo link to index.html.

Add a cart dropdown overlay component: when the .cart-btn is clicked, show a dropdown panel positioned below the cart button (position: absolute, z-index above content). The dropdown should show sample cart items (Glitch Hoodie $128, Static Tee $64, Loop Cargo $142), quantity controls, a subtotal, and a 'Checkout' button. Clicking outside or clicking the cart button again closes it. Add the CSS for this dropdown in css/styles.css and the JS toggle logic in js/main.js.

Update the New Arrivals section in index.html: add size/inventory badges below each product card's meta section. Each card should show available sizes (e.g., 'L', 'S', 'XS', 'XL', 'XXL') and mark some as 'out of inventory' (crossed out/grayed). Use the existing .size and .size.soldout classes from pages.css. Give each card different inventory patterns so it looks realistic.
Ensure the 'Submit yours' button on product.html (in the #community section-head) has href='studio.html' - it already does, just verify.

Modernize the homepage hero section: add a subtle animated gradient background that shifts colors slowly (dusty pink to dark), add a larger visual presence for the KREEP logo in the hero with the glitch effect active. Keep the existing hero structure (copy + floating cards) but enhance visual polish.
Add CSS for the new cart dropdown in css/styles.css. Style it with the existing design tokens (ink-800 background, line borders, dusty-pink accents, pill border-radius on buttons).
Ensure all existing interactions still work: glitch bursts on .glitch elements, animated counters, chip selection, PDP gallery, studio sliders/drag, mobile nav burger.
acceptance_criteria: SVG KREEP logo appears in the navbar of all 3 pages and in the hero of index.html with working glitch animation
Cart button click opens a dropdown overlay with items, quantities, subtotal, and checkout button; clicking outside or toggling closes it
New Arrivals section shows 4 product cards each with size availability chips (some marked soldout/out of inventory)
Submit yours button on product.html navigates to studio.html
Hero section has enhanced visual design with animated gradient background
All existing JS interactions remain functional (counters, drag, sliders, ticker, chip selection, glitch bursts)
verification: Open index.html - verify SVG logo in navbar with glitch effect, hero has logo and animated gradient
Click cart button - verify dropdown appears with items and closes on second click or outside click
Scroll to New Arrivals - verify each product card shows size chips with inventory status
Open product.html - click 'Submit yours' button and verify it navigates to studio.html
Open studio.html - verify SVG logo in navbar, existing sliders and drag still work
Verify glitch animation fires periodically on logo elements (every ~2.6s as per existing JS)
findings: All acceptance criteria satisfied. SVG logo with glitch class applied to .nav-logo wrapper on all 3 pages + hero. Cart dropdown uses .open class toggled via JS on cart-btn click with outside-click-to-close. Size chips added to all 4 New Arrivals cards using existing .size/.size.soldout classes. Hero gradient uses @keyframes heroGradient with background-size 400%. Submit yours button already linked to studio.html (confirmed). No build/test tooling exists for this static site - verification is visual/browser-based only.
